read-only.description: Read files, inspect docs, query the local backlog/audit databases, and run non-mutating checks.

generated-artifact.description: Regenerate docs, database mirrors, reports, and audit-safe artifacts.

code-change.description: Edit application, test, script, docs, and config files in the local repo.

migration.description: Create or modify database migrations or schema-affecting code.

secrets.description: Read, write, rotate, export, or reference secret material.

billing.description: Mutate billing, payment, plan, discount, or invoice state.

production.description: Deploy, push, run production commands, or alter production/provider state.

destructive.description: Delete, reset, force-push, wipe, drop, or irreversibly mutate state.

tierMeanings.asserted: A human or agent claim with no artifact. Never enough to sign off.
tierMeanings.config: The controlling configuration exists (Terraform / env / flag). Not sufficient for a sensitive release class.
tierMeanings.test: An automated check passed (unit / integration / gate).
tierMeanings.live: Observed true in the running or deployed system (probe, response header, measured metric, restore drill).

evidence.releaseGateRule: A backlog item whose feature carries a release-class:<class> label cannot be signed off (status 'verified') on asserted/config/test evidence alone; it requires a backlog event whose evidence_tier is 'live'. Enforced by the release_gate_violations view and npm run backlog:validate.
